title: 0/1 Knapsack
description: You have n items, each with weight w_i and value v_i. You have a knapsack with capacity W. Pick items to maximize total value without exceeding capacity. Each item can be taken at most once.
input_spec: The first line contains two integers n and W (1 <= n <= 1000, 1 <= W <= 1000). Then n lines follow, each with two integers w_i and v_i (1 <= w_i, v_i <= 1000).

routes.name: 2D DP table
routes.description: Build a table dp[i][w] = max value using first i items with capacity w.

observations.title: Take-or-skip recurrence
observations.description: For each item, you either take it (if it fits) or skip it. dp[i][w] = max(dp[i-1][w], dp[i-1][w-w_i] + v_i).

hints.nudge: For each item, what are your two choices?
hints.question: If you're deciding about item i with remaining capacity w, what are the two cases to consider?
hints.formal: dp[i][w] = max(dp[i-1][w] (skip), dp[i-1][w-w_i] + v_i (take, if w >= w_i)). Base: dp[0][*] = 0.

observations.title: Base case: no items, no value
observations.description: dp[0][w] = 0 for all w. With zero items, the maximum value is always 0.

hints.question: If you have 0 items to choose from, what's the max value for any capacity?
hints.formal: dp[0][w] = 0 for all 0 <= w <= W. This is the base row from which everything builds.

observations.title: Space optimization: 1D array with reverse iteration
observations.description: Since dp[i] only depends on dp[i-1], you can use a 1D array and iterate w from W down to w_i.

hints.nudge: Do you need the entire 2D table, or just the previous row?
hints.question: dp[i][w] uses dp[i-1][w] and dp[i-1][w-w_i]. Can you overwrite the array in-place if you iterate carefully?
hints.formal: Use 1D dp[w]. For each item, iterate w from W down to w_i: dp[w] = max(dp[w], dp[w-w_i]+v_i). Reverse order prevents using item twice.
